title: Gapless. Properly gapless.
body: Nanosecond-anchored scheduling on the same audio node. Live albums and DJ mixes flow without that little hiccup other players sneak past you.

title: Plays the awkward stuff.
body: FLAC, ALAC, MP3, AAC, OGG, Opus, WavPack, Monkey's Audio, DSD. Bit-perfect output to whatever DAC you've got plugged in.

title: Smart playlists with teeth.
body: Real predicates, not hand-wavy ones. "Played fewer than three times since 2019, rated four stars or higher, not in any other smart list." Done.

title: Tags it knows; tags you don't.
body: AcoustID fingerprinting hands you MusicBrainz IDs for that 64 kbps Limp Bizkit b-side from 2003 your friend insists is yours.

title: Mini-player that doesn't lie.
body: Stays on top, sized like a Winamp window, with a real spectrum analyser. No fake Apple-Music-style "oscilloscope". Reads the actual signal.

title: Lyrics, scrolling, synced.
body: LRC files, embedded lyrics, or fetched from Last.fm. Karaoke-style line highlight. Toggle a translucent lyrics pane next to the queue.

title: Cross-fade the mood.
body: Per-genre fade durations. Equal-power curves. The dance set blends, the classical doesn't. You set the rules once.

title: Scrobbles only when you say so.
body: Last.fm and ListenBrainz, opt-in, batched, signed. No background telemetry, no "anonymous usage stats". Bòcan never phones home.

title: Casts to AirPlay and Chromecast.
body: Pick a HomePod, a Sonos, a TV, or a cheap Chromecast Audio you forgot you owned. Volume control follows the focused window.

title: The Homer of music players.
body: Every cool feature from iTunes 9, Winamp 5, Foobar2000, and Cog — without the bloat, without the spyware, without the cloud.
